policy_text:
Effective June 1, 2026, all employees based in our San Francisco and New York offices are required to work from the office Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday each week. This policy applies to all departments and levels.

We believe in-person collaboration is essential to building the products our customers need and the culture we want to be known for. The era of indefinite remote flexibility is ending, and we are aligning with industry best practices.

Exceptions will be reviewed case-by-case at manager discretion. Employees who cannot meet the in-office requirement should discuss alternatives with their manager.

We are confident this change will accelerate execution, strengthen mentorship, and improve company performance. We appreciate your partnership in this transition.

parsed_policy.summary: San Francisco and New York office employees must work in-office Tuesday through Thursday each week starting June 1, 2026.

actions.content: I was hired explicitly as remote from Portland because I'm caring for my mom with early dementia. This policy puts me in an impossible position — I can't relocate, and I can't leave her. I need to understand what 'manager discretion' actually means for my situation.

The Tuesday-Thursday mandate puts me on a train 4 hours a day from Sacramento — that's half my productive time gone. I need to understand if there's any flexibility here before I make some hard calls about my future.
I'm genuinely excited about this. Our newer reps need more real-time coaching and deal collaboration than Zoom can deliver, and frankly we've been losing that muscle as a team.

Need to flag something urgent — the Tue-Thu mandate doesn't work with my Metro-North schedule and daycare pickup by 6pm. My husband travels weekly and I'd need to hire full-time help we can't afford. Can we talk about what 'manager discretion' actually means here?

We need to talk about the Tuesday-Thursday requirement before this goes live. My husband works night shifts in the ER and I have a 90-minute commute from Petaluma — those three days are literally impossible with two young kids, and we negotiated my current arrangement specificall

I need to talk as soon as possible. I was hired remote five years ago specifically because my kids need therapies only available here in Boulder — SF three days a week isn't something I can make work without destroying their care plan.

Need to talk through the Tuesday-Thursday mandate — between my parent in Jersey City and the kids' schedules, losing three remote days is going to create some real logistics problems. Is there flexibility on the specific days, or are we locked to Tue/Wed/Thu?

I think this is the right call — we've been bleeding cash on underutilized office space, and the collaboration gains will more than offset the adjustment period. Happy to help any direct reports work through logistics if needed.

I know this is going to be an adjustment, but I want to share why I'm supportive of the Tue-Thu in-office plan. Our sprint velocity and cross-team collaboration have measurably declined over the past year, and I believe intentional in-person time will help us rebuild the engineer
